reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Senior Salesforce Developer with 7 years in enterprise CRM platforms (Apex, LWC, Flow, Copado) and has zero evidence of embedded systems, C programming, MISRA guidelines, AUTOSAR, RTOS, or RADAR expertise — every single must-have for this role is missing. The JD demands an embedded/automotive software engineer with deep AUTOSAR and RTOS experience for what is clearly an ADAS/RADAR development role based in Chennai. The candidate's ECE B.Tech background (2014-2018) is the only tangential connection, but 7 years of Salesforce CRM work has taken them in a completely opposite career direction. Do not proceed — this is a domain mismatch, not a skills gap.
strengths: SALESFORCE DELIVERY LEADERSHIP: Led end-to-end Salesforce org delivery for 1500+ users at IBM/Informa, owning design through deployment using Copado — high-signal enterprise delivery credential, irrelevant to this role.

QUANTIFIED TEAM IMPACT: Mentored 5 developers and reduced deployment defects by 30%, demonstrating credible senior-level engineering influence — strong signal for a Salesforce role, not transferable here.

CERTIFICATION DEPTH: Holds 7 Salesforce certifications plus Azure AZ-900 and Copado credentials, indicating disciplined professional development — entirely misaligned with embedded/automotive domain requirements.
gaps: ZERO EMBEDDED SYSTEMS EXPERIENCE: The role mandates C programming with MISRA compliance, AUTOSAR stack knowledge, and RTOS proficiency — the candidate has no evidence of any embedded or automotive software development in their entire career history.

NO ADAS OR RADAR DOMAIN KNOWLEDGE: Core role deliverables involve RADAR system and algorithm development within an ADAS context — the candidate's 7-year Salesforce CRM career provides no foundation whatsoever for this work.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period specified in resume and JD has no maximum notice period constraint.
Candidate location is not explicitly stated in the resume, so no location penalty can be applied.
Candidate has 7+ years total experience, well above the 2-year minimum threshold (half of 4 years required).
JD gender preference is 'Any', so no penalty applies.
No college preference or exclusion specified in the JD.

availability_reason: Current role at IBM shows no end date and no notice period is stated in the resume.
rare_assets: Salesforce Certified AI Specialist — an early-mover credential in the Salesforce AI space that very few practitioners currently hold.
Copado DevOps certification paired with hands-on Copado deployment ownership across 500+ Apex classes — rare operational depth.
self_reported_unverified: Claimed 30% reduction in deployment defects after mentoring 5 developers — no baseline metrics or third-party validation provided.
Claims production ownership and critical defect management for a 1500+ user org — scope and severity of incidents not quantified.
interview_flags: Probe whether the ECE engineering background (2014-2018) included any embedded C or RTOS coursework that might indicate latent transferable skills.
Ask why a candidate with strong Salesforce credentials applied to an embedded AUTOSAR/RADAR role — potential CV misdirection or application error worth clarifying.
Verify the actual Copado usage depth — was it configuration-only or did they contribute to pipeline architecture and branching strategy design?
Probe the '500+ classes with 90%+ coverage' claim — ask for specifics on test architecture to assess genuine quality versus coverage gaming.
Clarify current notice period and whether candidate is actively exploring a domain switch or this is a mistaken application.
